questions.question: Who is Mme Loisel?
questions.correct_answer: The central character of the story who desires wealth and luxury
questions.explanation: Mme Loisel is a dissatisfied woman whose longing for a luxurious life leads to her downfall.

questions.question: Why is Mme Loisel unhappy with her life?
questions.correct_answer: She feels she deserves a richer and more luxurious life
questions.explanation: Her constant comparison with wealthy people makes her discontented.

questions.question: What does the invitation to the ball represent?
questions.correct_answer: An opportunity for Mme Loisel to display wealth and status
questions.explanation: The invitation excites Mme Loisel because it allows her to live her dream of luxury for one night.

questions.question: Why does Mme Loisel borrow the necklace?
questions.correct_answer: She wants to appear rich and elegant at the ball
questions.explanation: She believes jewellery is necessary to gain admiration and respect.

questions.question: What happens to the necklace after the ball?
questions.explanation: The loss of the necklace becomes the turning point of the story.

questions.question: How do the Loisels replace the necklace?
questions.correct_answer: They buy a new one by borrowing a large amount of money
questions.explanation: Mr Loisel spends his savings and borrows heavily to replace the necklace.

questions.question: What change occurs in Mme Loisel’s life after losing the necklace?
questions.correct_answer: She lives a life of poverty and hard labour for ten years
questions.explanation: The debt forces her into a harsh and exhausting lifestyle.

questions.question: What is ironic about the ending of the story?
questions.correct_answer: The necklace was fake and not valuable
questions.explanation: Years of suffering were caused by a necklace that was worth very little.

questions.question: What major theme does the story highlight?
questions.explanation: The story shows how false appearances can be misleading and destructive.

questions.question: What message does the story convey to readers?
questions.correct_answer: Contentment and honesty lead to true happiness
questions.explanation: The story warns against pride, materialism, and dishonesty.

notes.title: Pride and Materialism
notes.content: Mme Loisel’s pride and desire for luxury are the root causes of her suffering.

notes.title: Role of Irony
notes.content: Irony strengthens the moral message by revealing the true value of the necklace at the end.

facts.fact: The story shows how a single mistake can change an entire life.
Guy de Maupassant often used irony to criticise materialism in society.
